display.purpose: Dashboard hero metrics (text-display); pairs with mono tabular numerics.

headline.purpose: Page titles, rendered through the PageHeader primitive.

body.purpose: The workhorse — table cells, form fields, copy. Prose at 65-75ch; dense tables may run wider.

label.purpose: Uppercase eyebrows and nav section headings (text-label), used sparingly.

mono.purpose: Geist Mono for identifiers, metrics, and tabular numerics.

shadows.name: e1
shadows.purpose: Resting cards, panels, the DetailPanel. The default surface elevation. Dark mode adds an inset top-highlight.

shadows.name: e2
shadows.purpose: Hover/focus on interactive cards — the lift target.

shadows.name: e1-dark
shadows.purpose: Dark-mode e1 — same shape at ~2x alpha plus a bone top-highlight so raised surfaces catch light on near-black.

motion.name: ease-out-quad
motion.purpose: The workhorse easing — settles, never bounces. Default for transitions and entrances.

motion.name: ease-spring
motion.purpose: Overshoots once. RESERVED for the success checkmark only — do not borrow for general motion.

motion.name: dur-slow
motion.purpose: Entrances (rise-in / fade-in), the cross-route settle, and the check-draw.

motion.name: rise-in
motion.value: @keyframes rise-in { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(12px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: none; } }
motion.purpose: Component-level surface entrance — 12px rise + fade. Use on cards/rows; never at the route root (transform traps sticky descendants).

motion.name: fade-in
motion.purpose: Opacity-only route-level settle — no transform, so it never establishes a containing block for sticky descendants. app/(app)/template.tsx wraps each route in this.

motion.name: pulse-soft
motion.purpose: The ONE calm attention heartbeat (2.4s) — danger dots only.

motion.name: shimmer
motion.purpose: Skeleton sweep, composed onto the muted skeleton fill at call sites (never edit ui/skeleton.tsx).

motion.name: check-draw
motion.purpose: Success-check stroke draw — the single ease-spring use.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single primary action per view — indigo fill, used under the One Voice Rule.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; background: var(--primary, oklch(0.55 0.18 275)); color: var(--primary-foreground, oklch(0.985 0 0)); font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25rem; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: none; border-radius: 0.5rem; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px oklch(0.21 0.006 75 / 0.06); cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94), filter 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.06); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ring, oklch(0.55 0.18 275)); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { filter: brightness(0.96); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: A quiet, neutral-fill action for everything that isn't the one primary.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; background: var(--muted, oklch(0.955 0.005 95)); color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.21 0.006 75)); font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25rem; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: 1px solid var(--border, oklch(0.9 0.005 95)); border-radius: 0.5rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: oklch(0.93 0.005 95); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ring, oklch(0.55 0.18 275)); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: A solid-fill semantic status pill (StatusBadge) — readable on the bone because it fills solid, never tints text. Includes a leading dot so color is not the sole carrier of meaning.
components.css: .ds-status-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.375rem; background: var(--success, oklch(0.53 0.14 150)); color: var(--success-foreground, oklch(0.985 0 0)); font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1rem; letter-spacing: 0.01em; padding: 0.125rem 0.5rem; border-radius: 0.375rem; } .ds-status-dot { width: 0.375rem; height: 0.375rem; border-radius: 9999px; background: currentColor; }

components.name: Card
components.description: An object on the bench — raised a hair above the bone, resting at e1, lifting to e2 on hover via the coordinated lift triad. No nested cards.
components.html:
MacBook Pro 14"

Assigned to Dana Ruiz · Floor 3
components.css: .ds-card { background: var(--card, oklch(0.995 0.003 95)); color: var(--card-foreground, oklch(0.21 0.006 75)); border: 1px solid var(--border, oklch(0.9 0.005 95)); border-radius: 0.875rem; padding: 1.5rem; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px oklch(0.21 0.006 75 / 0.06), 0 1px 1px oklch(0.21 0.006 75 / 0.04); transition: transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94), box-shadow 180ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94); } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 4px 12px -2px oklch(0.21 0.006 75 / 0.1), 0 2px 4px -2px oklch(0.21 0.006 75 / 0.06); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-card { transition: box-shadow 0.01ms; } .ds-card:hover { transform: none; } } .ds-card-title { font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.5rem; letter-spacing: -0.01em; margin: 0 0 0.25rem; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.25rem; color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.52 0.008 75)); margin: 0; }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: A calm field with an inline search glyph that comes alive on focus with the indigo ring — no glow, no glassmorphism.
components.css: .ds-field { position: relative; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; width: 100%; max-width: 20rem; } .ds-field-icon { position: absolute; left: 0.625rem; width: 1rem; height: 1rem; color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.52 0.008 75)); pointer-events: none; } .ds-input { width: 100%; background: var(--card, oklch(0.995 0.003 95)); color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.21 0.006 75)); font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.25rem; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem 0.5rem 2rem; border: 1px solid var(--border, oklch(0.9 0.005 95)); border-radius: 0.5rem; outline: none; transition: border-color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94), box-shadow 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.52 0.008 75)); } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: var(--ring, oklch(0.55 0.18 275)); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.55 0.18 275 / 0.25); }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item (active)
components.description: A sidebar entry in its active state — a muted tint plus the indigo icon and a 2px state rule, so wayfinding is quiet and never carried by color alone.
components.html: Assets
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.625rem; font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25rem; color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.21 0.006 75)); padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; text-decoration: none; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94); } .ds-nav-icon { width: 1.125rem; height: 1.125rem; flex: none; color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.52 0.008 75)); } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: var(--sidebar-accent, oklch(0.94 0.006 95)); } .ds-nav-item--active { background: var(--muted, oklch(0.955 0.005 95)); color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.21 0.006 75)); font-weight: 600; box-shadow: inset 2px 0 0 var(--primary, oklch(0.55 0.18 275)); } .ds-nav-item--active .ds-nav-icon { color: var(--primary, oklch(0.55 0.18 275)); } .ds-nav-item:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ring, oklch(0.55 0.18 275)); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Empty State
components.description: The warm 'nothing here yet' surface — a pillar-tinted icon chip plus a one-line invitation and an action. Teaches the interface instead of saying 'nothing here'.
components.html:
Nothing here yet

Add your first asset and it shows up here.
components.css: .ds-empty { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; text-align: center; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 2.5rem 1.5rem; animation: ds-rise-in 220ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94) both; } @keyframes ds-rise-in { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(12px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: none; } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-empty { animation-duration: 0.01ms; } } .ds-empty-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 3rem; height: 3rem; border-radius: 9999px; margin-bottom: 0.25rem; background: oklch(0.62 0.14 200 / 0.1); color: var(--pillar-inventory, oklch(0.62 0.14 200)); } .ds-empty-chip svg { width: 1.5rem; height: 1.5rem; } .ds-empty-title { font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.5rem; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.21 0.006 75)); margin: 0; } .ds-empty-body { font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.25rem; color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.52 0.008 75)); margin: 0 0 0.5rem; } .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; background: var(--primary, oklch(0.55 0.18 275)); color: var(--primary-foreground, oklch(0.985 0 0)); font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25rem; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: none; border-radius: 0.5rem; cursor: pointer; transition: filter 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.06); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ring, oklch(0.55 0.18 275)); outline-offset: 2px; }

narrative.overview: lazyit is the bench of a craftsperson who keeps a tidy shop. The canvas is a warm bone — paper on a desk, not a white screen — and the surfaces on it are objects you can pick up: cards lift when you reach for them, shadows fall warm like afternoon light, and a single disciplined indigo marks the one tool you're holding. Color is the labelled drawer (teal for Inventory, indigo for Access, green for Knowledge, rose for Manage), never wallpaper. The system is calm because the operator is mid-task and the work, not the chrome, deserves their attention — but it is alive: things move, settle, and layer with intention. This is the difference between a cared-for workshop and an austere SaaS. Density is earned, not feared. This is the product register: when an operator needs a table of 200 assets or a panel of forty labels, the system gives it to them densely and legibly — tabular numerics, a tight type scale, consistent affordances screen to screen. The energy never comes from louder buttons or more CTAs; it comes from motion, depth, and color identity layered onto an otherwise quiet surface. Restraint is the floor; craft is how we activate it. The Calm Workshop explicitly rejects the things that make internal tools feel un-cared-for: the generic AI-slop dashboard, heavy/dated ServiceNow, SaaS landing-page clichés, glassmorphism, gradient text, tiny uppercase eyebrows on every section, the hollow hero-metric template, and austere SaaS coldness. It does not chase delight on every page — delight is saved for moments (a success checkmark, an empty state that invites), never sprayed across the surface.
keyCharacteristics: Warm-bone canvas, never #fff/#000 — a committed brand decision (ADR-0011), not a default.
One disciplined indigo for action, selection, and state — ≤10% of any screen.
Four pillar hues as a labelling layer — tint/chip/dot/border only, never as readable text.
A warm, named elevation scale — objects rest at e1 and lift on interaction.
A single CSS motion vocabulary — 120–220ms, state-conveying, reduced-motion-safe, zero JS lib.
One type family (Geist + Geist Mono) on a fixed rem scale — dense when the task is dense.
WCAG-AA by construction in both themes; color never carries meaning alone.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Brand indigo is reserved for action, selection, and state — primary buttons, focus rings, the active nav item — and used on ≤10% of any given screen. Its rarity is the point. Indigo is never decoration, never a section background, never a 'let's add some color here' flourish.

rules.name: The Pillar-as-Decoration Rule
rules.body: A pillar or chart hue may be a tint, a border, a dot, or a chip background — never readable text on bone or on a /10 tint. Each pillar token aliases a --chart-* hue (which .dark redefines lighter), so it cannot clear 4.5:1 as body text. Decorative glyphs ≥24px inside a bg-pillar-*/10 chip are exempt; everything readable stays on --foreground / --card-foreground or a semantic *-foreground.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Geist (and Geist Mono for numerics/identifiers) carries the entire UI. No display face, no second sans, never a display font in a label, button, or data cell. The discipline is the IT-native voice.

rules.name: The Warm-Paper Rule
rules.body: Shadows are warm-tinted (the foreground hue), never neutral gray — paper on a desk, lit by afternoon light. Surfaces rest at e1 and lift on interaction. The signature is the lift recipe: the coordinated hover triad -translate-y-0.5 + e1→e2 + ring /10→/15, fired together, so a card hover feels crafted, not a flat background swap. Never apply lift to static table rows. Audit test: if a card looks like a 2014 app, the shadow is too dark, too tight, and too gray — warm it and spread it.

dos: Do keep brand indigo to ≤10% of a screen — action, selection, and state only (The One Voice Rule).
Do express pillar identity via a tinted icon chip (bg-pillar-*/10 + an aria-hidden glyph), a dot, or a subtle background tint.
Do rest surfaces at shadow-e1 and lift on interaction with the lift recipe (-translate-y-0.5 + e1→e2 + ring /10→/15).
Do keep all motion in CSS + tw-animate-css, 150–250ms, conveying state, and reduced-motion-safe.
Do treat the warm --bone canvas as a deliberate, committed brand decision (ADR-0011); warmth is also carried by motion, color identity, and personality.
Do give metric cards real substance — deep-linked breakdowns, tabular numerics — not a hollow number.
Do keep readable text on --foreground / --card-foreground, or use a semantic StatusBadge solid-fill with its AA-verified *-foreground.
Do ship the full state set on every interactive component (default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading) and the same vocabulary screen to screen.
donts: Don't lean on SaaS landing clichés.
Don't put tiny uppercase eyebrows on every section.
Don't ship the hollow hero-metric template.

Don't use border-left / border-right greater than 1px as a colored pillar accent on cards, rows, callouts, or alerts (an absolute ban). The narrow exception: a ≤2px active-nav selection rule is allowed as a state indicator — but prefer bg-tint + weight + icon color.

Don't add a JS motion library (framer-motion, gsap, anime, lenis). No orchestrated page-load choreography: the route settle is a subtle opacity fade, and the metric stagger is minimal and initial-mount only.
Don't read the warm --bone canvas as the 'AI cream default' — it is a committed brand decision (ADR-0011), not a fallback.
Don't use a pillar/chart hue as readable text on bone or on a /10 tint. Keep text on --foreground / --card-foreground or a semantic StatusBadge solid-fill.
